application_summary.name: Allegro - Java Swing MVP Personal Data Form
application_summary.description: A Java Swing desktop application implementing the Model-View-Presenter (MVP) architectural pattern. It presents a personal data entry form with German-language labels, collects user input across 13 fields covering personal identity, address, banking information, gender classification, and a validity date. On submission it serialises all values to a JSON POST request sent to a local REST endpoint, displays the server response in a text area, and resets all form fields.
application_summary.purpose: Proof-of-concept desktop form demonstrating MVP pattern in Java Swing with real-time two-way data binding, an observer-based event system, and HTTP REST integration.
key_functionalities: Personal data entry form with German-language labels (Vorname, Name, Geburtsdatum, PLZ, Ort, Strasse, IBAN, BIC, Gültig ab)
Real-time two-way data binding between View components and Model ValueModel entries
Mutually exclusive gender radio buttons: Weiblich (Female), Männlich (Male), Divers (Diverse)
JSON POST submission of all 13 form fields to http://localhost:8080/post
Server response displayed in a JTextArea
Automatic full form reset after successful server response
Observer pattern event bus (EventEmitter/EventListener) decoupling Model from Presentation
MVP architectural separation: PocModel, PocView, PocPresenter

packages.name: com.poc
packages.description: Root package — generic reusable utility classes shared across layers

packages.name: com.poc.model
packages.description: Model layer — domain field enum, value containers, event system, HTTP service, and business orchestration

packages.name: com.poc.presentation
packages.description: Presentation layer — Swing UI view and MVP presenter with bidirectional data bindings

files.summary: Generic single-field value container — the atomic data-holding unit for every model property in the MVP model map.
files.description: ValueModel<T> is a generic wrapper encapsulating one typed value. It is used as the value type in PocModel's EnumMap, allowing each of the 13 ModelProperties keys to store either a String or Boolean. The Presenter retrieves each ValueModel instance from the map and updates its field via setField() on every UI event. PocModel reads field values via getField() during submission.
business_rules: Each form field is represented by exactly one ValueModel instance keyed by a ModelProperties enum constant
ValueModel<String> is used for all text-based fields; ValueModel<Boolean> is used for gender radio buttons (MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE)
All ValueModel instances are initialised with null values at model construction time
Model values are synchronised in real time by the Presenter on every document or change event

method_breakdown.summary: Constructs a ValueModel with an initial value (null at startup for all model properties)
parameters: field (T): initial value; null for all 13 properties at construction time
method_breakdown.significance: Called 13 times by PocModel constructor to initialise every model property slot

method_breakdown.returns: T: current field value (String or Boolean depending on property type)
method_breakdown.significance: Invoked by PocModel.action() to collect all values for HTTP submission; null values at submission time will cause NullPointerException
Invoked by PocPresenter bindings on every keystroke (text fields) or selection change (radio buttons) to maintain model-view synchronisation

files.summary: Functional interface defining the callback contract for receiving events from the EventEmitter observer bus.
files.description: EventListener is a single-abstract-method interface enabling lambda implementations. It decouples the Model layer (which emits events after HTTP operations) from the Presentation layer (which reacts to them). The String eventData payload carries the server response body or a failure message.
business_rules: Any consumer of model results must implement onEvent(String eventData)
Event payload is a raw String — either the HTTP response body or the literal 'Failed operation' string

method_breakdown.summary: Callback invoked when the EventEmitter broadcasts an event
parameters: eventData (String): server response body or 'Failed operation' status string
method_breakdown.significance: Implemented as a lambda in PocPresenter to update the text area and reset all form fields after submission

files.summary: Observer-pattern event bus that manages a list of EventListener subscribers and broadcasts String events to all of them.
files.description: EventEmitter maintains an ArrayList of EventListener instances. The subscribe() method adds a listener; the emit() method iterates all listeners and calls onEvent() on each. This allows PocModel to notify PocPresenter of submission outcomes without holding a direct reference to the view layer.
business_rules: Multiple listeners can subscribe; all receive every emitted event in registration order
If HTTP response body is non-empty, the response body string is emitted
If HTTP response body is empty, the string 'Failed operation' is emitted
The event bus is shared between PocModel (emitter) and PocPresenter (subscriber) via constructor injection

method_breakdown.summary: Registers a listener to receive all future events
parameters: listener (EventListener): subscriber to add; in practice the PocPresenter lambda
method_breakdown.significance: Called in PocPresenter constructor to register the view-reset and response-display callback

method_breakdown.summary: Broadcasts eventData to every registered listener
parameters: eventData (String): payload to broadcast — HTTP response body or 'Failed operation'
method_breakdown.significance: Called by PocModel.action() as the final step of the submission workflow, triggering UI updates in the Presentation layer

files.summary: Enum defining all 13 domain data fields of the personal data form, serving as type-safe keys for the model's EnumMap.
files.description: ModelProperties enumerates every attribute captured by the Allegro form. It is used as the key type of PocModel's EnumMap<ModelProperties, ValueModel<?>>, as the target of all PocPresenter bind() calls, and as the field name in the JSON payload sent to the backend. The enum covers personal identity (FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH), address (ZIP, ORT, STREET), banking (IBAN, BIC), temporal validity (VALID_FROM), response display (TEXT_AREA), and gender classification (FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE).
business_rules: All 13 properties must be present in the model map before the Presenter attaches bindings
Gender is represented by three mutually exclusive Boolean flags: FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE
TEXT_AREA is used both as a free-text input and as the server response display field
Enum name strings (e.g. 'FIRST_NAME') become the JSON field keys in the HTTP payload
Address data requires all three fields: ZIP (Postleitzahl), ORT (city), STREET (Strasse)
Banking data requires both IBAN and BIC
DATE_OF_BIRTH and VALID_FROM are stored as plain strings without format validation in this implementation

files.summary: Empty placeholder DTO — currently contains no fields or methods; represents a design intention for a view state data transfer object.
files.description: ViewData is an unimplemented stub class. Its package placement (com.poc.model) and naming suggest it is intended to carry a snapshot of view state between layers or as a parameter object. No business logic or fields have been added. It has no impact on the current runtime behaviour of the application.

files.summary: HTTP client service that serialises a String key-value map into a JSON object body and POSTs it to a local REST endpoint, returning the raw response body.
files.description: HttpBinService encapsulates all HTTP communication. It opens a POST connection to http://localhost:8080/post, streams a JSON object built from the supplied map using javax.json's streaming API, reads and returns the response body as a String. Response code and body are printed to stdout. The class is instantiated directly by PocModel (no interface, no injection).
business_rules: All 13 form field values are submitted together as a single atomic JSON POST request
Target endpoint is always http://localhost:8080/post (no configurability at runtime)
Content-Type header must be application/json
JSON keys are ModelProperties enum name strings (e.g. FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME)
JSON values are string representations of field values (Boolean values become 'true'/'false')
A non-empty response body signals a successful operation; empty body signals failure
Response code and body are always logged to standard output

method_breakdown.summary: Serialises the provided field map to JSON and POSTs to the REST endpoint, returning the raw response body
parameters: data (Map<String,String>): map of 13 ModelProperties enum name strings to their current string values
method_breakdown.returns: String: raw HTTP response body from the server
method_breakdown.significance: Sole backend integration point; all form data flows through this single method; failure here causes an IOException propagated to the Presenter as a RuntimeException

files.summary: Central model class that owns all 13 form field ValueModels and orchestrates the complete submission workflow: data collection, HTTP POST, and event notification.
files.description: PocModel is the Model layer of the MVP triangle. Its constructor initialises an EnumMap with 13 ValueModel entries (10 String, 3 Boolean) all set to null. When action() is called by the Presenter, it: (1) logs all field values to stdout, (2) builds a String map keyed by ModelProperties enum names, (3) delegates the HTTP POST to HttpBinService, and (4) emits either the response body or 'Failed operation' via EventEmitter. PocModel has no direct reference to the View.
business_rules: All 13 form fields are null-initialised at application start; no default values are set
On submit action all current model field values are printed to stdout for debugging
All field values are converted to String via Object.toString() — null values will cause NullPointerException at submission unless all fields are populated by the user
If the HTTP response body is non-empty, it is emitted as the event payload
If the HTTP response body is empty, 'Failed operation' is emitted as the event payload
Gender Boolean fields (MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE) are serialised as 'true' or 'false' strings in the JSON payload

method_breakdown.summary: Initialises the 13-entry EnumMap with null-valued ValueModels and stores the event emitter reference
parameters: eventEmitter (EventEmitter): shared event bus used to broadcast submission outcomes
method_breakdown.significance: Must complete before the Presenter attaches bindings; establishes the full in-memory data model

method_breakdown.summary: Collects all model field values, POSTs them as JSON to the REST endpoint, emits the response or failure event
method_breakdown.significance: The single business workflow entry point; triggered by the Presenter's submit button ActionListener; all data collection and backend communication passes through this method

files.summary: Java Swing view declaring all 14 UI components of the personal data form with German labels, arranged using GridBagLayout in an 800x650 JFrame.
files.description: PocView constructs and displays the main application window ('Allegro'). It declares as protected fields: a JTextArea for response display, 9 JTextFields for personal and address/banking data, 3 JRadioButtons for gender selection (grouped in a ButtonGroup for mutual exclusivity), and a JButton labelled 'Anordnen'. The GridBagLayout provides structured positional alignment. Protected access to all component fields enables the Presenter to attach listeners and reset values directly.
business_rules: Application window title is 'Allegro'
Submit button label is 'Anordnen' (German: Arrange/Order)
Gender options are Weiblich (Female), Männlich (Male), Divers (Diverse)
All gender radio buttons belong to one ButtonGroup — only one can be selected at a time
Window dimensions are fixed at 800x650 pixels
Application process exits when the main window is closed (EXIT_ON_CLOSE)

method_breakdown.summary: Constructor that calls initUI() to build and display the Swing form
method_breakdown.significance: Entry point for view construction; must be called on the Event Dispatch Thread

method_breakdown.summary: Creates all Swing components, configures GridBagLayout constraints for each, assembles the form panel, and makes the JFrame visible
method_breakdown.significance: Defines the complete visual structure; the protected component references exposed here are the Presenter's only access points to the UI

files.summary: MVP Presenter that establishes real-time two-way bindings for all 13 form fields, handles the submit button action, and reacts to model events by updating the view.
files.description: PocPresenter is the coordinator of the MVP pattern. In its constructor it: (1) subscribes a lambda to the EventEmitter that displays the server response in the text area and clears all 10 text fields and resets gender to Female; (2) attaches an ActionListener to the submit button that calls model.action(); (3) calls initializeBindings() to wire all 13 view components to their model counterparts. The overloaded bind() methods use DocumentListeners for text components (syncing on insertUpdate and removeUpdate) and ChangeListeners for radio buttons.
business_rules: All 13 model properties are bound to their view components before any user interaction
Text field model values are updated on every document insert and remove event — real-time synchronisation
Radio button Boolean model values are updated on every change event
On form submit, the current state of all 13 bound model values is sent to the backend
On receiving a server event, the text area is populated with the response body
On receiving a server event, all 9 text input fields are cleared to empty string
On receiving a server event, gender resets to: Female=selected, Male=deselected, Diverse=deselected
Each bind() call initialises the model field with the component's current value at binding time (empty string for text fields)
IOException and InterruptedException from model.action() are wrapped and rethrown as RuntimeException
files.sub_capability: MVP Data Binding and Action Coordination

method_breakdown.method_name: PocPresenter(PocView view, PocModel model, EventEmitter eventEmitter)
method_breakdown.summary: Constructs the presenter, registers the event-driven view-reset callback, attaches the submit button listener, and wires all bindings
parameters: view (PocView): Swing form with all UI component references
model (PocModel): data model holding all 13 ValueModel entries
eventEmitter (EventEmitter): shared event bus for receiving post-submission outcomes
method_breakdown.significance: The single assembly point for the entire MVP triangle; constructs the full reactive pipeline from UI events to model updates to HTTP submission and back to UI

method_breakdown.summary: Wires a JTextComponent to a String ValueModel using a DocumentListener that syncs content on every insert and remove
parameters: source (JTextComponent): JTextField or JTextArea to observe
prop (ModelProperties): the model property key to update
method_breakdown.significance: Ensures zero data-loss binding for all 10 text-based fields; the Document getText() call reads the complete current content after every edit

method_breakdown.summary: Wires a JRadioButton to a Boolean ValueModel using a ChangeListener
parameters: source (JRadioButton): the radio button to observe (female, male, or diverse)
prop (ModelProperties): FEMALE, MALE, or DIVERSE model property key
method_breakdown.significance: Keeps all three gender Boolean flags accurately mirrored in the model; radio button mutual exclusivity is enforced by the ButtonGroup in the View

method_breakdown.summary: Calls bind() for all 13 view components mapping each to its ModelProperties constant
method_breakdown.significance: Completes the MVP wiring; must be called after EventEmitter subscription and button listener setup to guarantee all model slots are populated before any potential submission
